title: Asuka line in the fight with armisael
display_title: Asuka line in the fight with armisael
wikitext:
[]: Asuka line in the fight with armisael             Quote Post by zephyr72 » Tue May 29, 2007 3:50 am Does anybody remember this line, after Asuka proves useless in this particular battle, where she basically says that she hates the fact that Shinji was sent out to protect Rei, but not her during her mind rape episode. I find it kind of interesting that she is always saying she doesn`t want to be saved and goes practically suicidal/murderous everytime she loses (to Shinji), but she still basically says she wanted to be saved by Shinji. Why is this? PS I did do a thread search and didn`t come up with anything so please don`t hang me.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue May 29, 2007 4:19 am This bit? ep. 23 wrote: IKARI: The suspension on Unit 01 has been lifted as of now. Send him out immediately. Send him out. Misato: Yes, Sir. Asuka: What? You didn't send him out for me.

[]: Re: Asuka line in the fight with armisael             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue May 29, 2007 7:13 am zephyr72 wrote: I find it kind of interesting that she is always saying she doesn`t want to be saved and goes practically suicidal/murderous everytime she loses (to Shinji), but she still basically says she wanted to be saved by Shinji. Why is this?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue May 29, 2007 12:46 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Perhaps it was just the thought she was offended by.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Tue May 29, 2007 12:51 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: Perhaps it was just the thought she was offended by.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed May 30, 2007 5:11 pm she just wanted to act like an adult, and didnt want anybodys help but she still is a child, who really does depend on other people (sometimes)

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 30, 2007 5:30 pm Having the starting stages of NPD* can do that to you. (What Asuka suffers from, which is a combination of self-worship and self-doubt.) Asuka WANTS Shinji's help and love, but at the same time, SHE still wants to be the best..... *sigh* They're so fucked up, thus different from other anime couples out there. THAT'S why I love 'em as a pairing! ^^; *More about this here: http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/narcissistic-personality-disorder/DS00652 I mean, come, just LOOK at this list and tell me this doesn't sound like Asuka (bolded by me): Signs and symptoms of narcissistic personality disorder may include: * Grandiose sense of one's own abilities or achievements * Fantasies about having exceptional power, attractiveness or success * Sense of belonging to an exclusive group of people who truly understand each other (Though Asuka belongs to an "exclusive group of people" Eva Pilots, she certinaly doesn't bother trying to understand them!) * Need for constant praise * Expectations of special treatment * Exploitation of other people * Lack of empathy for other people * Envy of other people or a belief that you are the subject of other people's envy * Haughty or arrogant behaviors

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed May 30, 2007 5:47 pm OTOH, having a one-in-a-billion talent might be taken as a reasonable excuse for all that.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Wed May 30, 2007 6:51 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Signs and symptoms of narcissistic personality disorder may include: * Grandiose sense of one's own abilities or achievements * Fantasies about having exceptional power, attractiveness or success * Sense of belonging to an exclusive group of people who truly understand each other (Though Asuka belongs to an "exclusive group of people" Eva Pilots, she certinaly doesn't bother trying to understand them!) * Need for constant praise * Expectations of special treatment * Exploitation of other people * Lack of empathy for other people * Envy of other people or a belief that you are the subject of other people's envy * Haughty or arrogant behaviors

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Wed May 30, 2007 6:53 pm Asuka always needed to be put in her place throughout the entirity of the series. Seriosly, she gets really annoying until epi. 22

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed May 30, 2007 6:54 pm Executor wrote: Asuka always needed to be put in her place throughout the entirity of the series. Seriosly, she gets really annoying until epi. 22

[]:             Quote Post by Executor » Wed May 30, 2007 7:00 pm And, yet, that is exactly why NERV, in almost any context, can't really be taken seriosly as a military organization. Aside from the children, if a military had somebody like Asuka, she'd difinatly get busted up by your local Gunnery Sargent Hartman, or flat-out reject, or kicked out of the military.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Wed May 30, 2007 7:00 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Part of her charm.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed May 30, 2007 7:39 pm Mr. Tines wrote: OTOH, having a one-in-a-billion talent might be taken as a reasonable excuse for all that.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed May 30, 2007 7:44 pm Tokpile Quohog wrote: Stardust-chan wrote: Symptoms of NPD

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed May 30, 2007 7:48 pm Executor wrote: And, yet, that is exactly why NERV, in almost any context, can't really be taken seriosly as a military organization. Aside from the children, if a military had somebody like Asuka, she'd difinatly get busted up by your local Gunnery Sargent Hartman, or flat-out reject, or kicked out of the military.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed May 30, 2007 9:42 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: OTOH, having a one-in-a-billion talent might be taken as a reasonable excuse for all that.

[]:             Quote Post by Big Panda » Wed May 30, 2007 11:01 pm I was on wikipedia (I know... I'm sorry...) and I found this: True self, false self Alexander Lowen describes pathological narcissism, and narcissistic personality disorder, as "the denial of the true self" Masterson describes the creation of a false self as: "When a young child fails to separate her own self-image from that of her mother. This happens roughly between the ages of two and three, often because of a parentâ€™s own emotional problems. A motherâ€™s encouragement of a childâ€™s self-assertion is vital. When the mother suffers from low self-esteem, she has difficulty encouraging her childâ€™s emerging self. The child experiences this absence as a loss of self, creating feelings of abandonment that lead to depression. To deal with the depression, the child gives up efforts to support her emerging self. Instead, she relies on her motherâ€™s approval to maintain the esteem of a 'false self'." --- Despite some few things, that frightfuly hit it. And since I realized that I suck to make a point in english, I'm gonna leave the obvious rest to you guys. Sry.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Wed May 30, 2007 11:30 pm I don't blame Asuka for being a bitch anymore than I blame Shinji for being a wuss (I really wanted to use a really bad word here, but decided against it). They don't need to make excuses for the way they are any more than other mentally ill people. It was just there messed up ways of coping with their respective god awful situations, so cut em a lil slack, kay? Seariouzly.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed May 30, 2007 11:50 pm TriLink12 wrote: I don't blame Asuka for being a bitch anymore than I blame Shinji for being a wuss (I really wanted to use a really bad word here, but decided against it). They don't need to make excuses for the way they are any more than other mentally ill people. It was just there messed up ways of coping with their respective god awful situations, so cut em a lil slack, kay? Seariouzly.
